articles.title: How Well Does AI-Generated Feedback Work? Intrinsic and Extrinsic Evaluation across more than 20,000 EFL Essay Drafts
articles.abstract: This study examines feedback in English as a Foreign Language (EFL) writing contexts, focusing on written corrective feedback (WCF). Large language models (LLMs) can provide WCF at scale, but aligning them with pedagogical best practices remains an ongoing challenge. WCF meeting criteria like factuality or relevance may still be unsuitable for learning contexts, highlighting the need for extrinsic evaluation based on the learner's perspective. We deployed WCF systems in a university-level EFL class with nearly 2,000 students, collecting over 20,000 drafts. We evaluated the generated WCF from two perspectives: intrinsic evaluation by experienced English teachers using a rubric, and extrinsic evaluation via student feedback and engagement metrics. Results revealed low alignment between teacher expert ratings and student feedback. These findings suggest that traditional expert evaluation alone may not fully capture WCF's usability or helpfulness from the learner's perspective, highlighting the importance of learner-centered evaluation frameworks for AI-based applications in language education.
articles.comment: Pre-review version of DOI https://doi.org/10.1007/978-3-032-29788-4_35, presented at AIED 2026 Late Breaking Results. Readers are encouraged to refer to the published version
articles.titleCn: 人工智能生成的反馈效果如何？超过20,000篇英语作为外语作文草稿的内在与外在评估
articles.abstractCn: 本研究考察了英语作为外语（EFL）写作环境中的反馈，重点关注书面纠正反馈（WCF）。大型语言模型（LLMs）能够大规模提供WCF，但将其与教学最佳实践相匹配仍是一个持续性的挑战。符合事实性或相关性等标准的WCF可能仍不适用于学习环境，这凸显了基于学习者视角进行外在评估的必要性。我们在一所拥有近2000名学生的大学英语EFL课堂部署了WCF系统，收集了超过20000份草稿。我们从两个角度评估了生成的WCF：由经验丰富的英语教师使用评分标准进行的内在评估，以及通过学生反馈和参与度指标进行的外在评估。结果表明，教师专家评分与学生反馈之间的一致性较低。这些发现表明，传统的专家评估可能无法全面捕捉WCF在学习者视角下的可用性或有效性，突显了在语言教育中基于人工智能的应用需要学习者中心评估框架的重要性。

articles.title: Qubes OS Security in the Public Record
articles.abstract: Qubes OS is a revealing case for security measurement because its architecture makes component boundaries security-relevant. We present a protocol-driven longitudinal analysis of 109 public Qubes Security Bulletins (QSBs, 2011--2025), the official Qubes-maintained Xen Security Advisory (XSA) tracker, and a secondary vulnerability-event sensitivity series. The study measures the public advisory record rather than latent vulnerability incidence or realized compromise. The methodology combines audited deterministic component attribution, change-point analysis, overdispersion checks, severity-proxy weighting, censoring sensitivity, documentary latency lower bounds, and baseline-aware evaluation of vulnerability discovery models (VDMs). The results show persistent upstream dependence in that public record. On the official tracker, 113 of 464 XSAs affect Qubes; under primary labeling, 87 of 109 QSBs (79.8\%) are attributable to Xen, CPU/microarchitectural, or other upstream components rather than Qubes-core logic, with similar results under weighted views. Change-point analyses identify 2015Q1 as the dominant break in the quarterly advisory series, while post-2018 annual disclosure rates are statistically flat. Poisson inferences are stable under dispersion diagnostics and negative-binomial sensitivity checks. The attribution codebook performs well in a stratified 30-QSB audit, and S-shaped VDMs fit descriptively but do not significantly outperform a rolling-mean baseline in short-horizon forecasts. Overall, the Qubes public advisory record appears stable, but not quiet: disclosure activity plateaus at a higher level than in the earliest years, while the observed burden remains concentrated in upstream trust anchors.
articles.comment: 18 pages, 3 figures, 4 tables, dataset bundle published on Zenodo
articles.titleCn: Qubes OS 公开记录中的安全性
articles.abstractCn: Qubes OS是安全度量的一个典型案例，因为其架构使得组件边界具有安全相关性。我们通过分析109份公开的Qubes Security Bulletins（QSBs，2011—2025年）、官方维护的Qubes Xen Security Advisory（XSA）追踪器以及次级漏洞事件敏感性系列，进行了一种协议驱动的纵向分析。该研究衡量的是公开公告记录，而非潜在的漏洞发生率或实际发生的入侵。研究方法结合了经过审计的确定性组件归因、变化点分析、过度分散检验、严重性代理加权、删失敏感性、文件记录滞后下限以及基于基线的漏洞发现模型（VDMs）评估。结果表明，在公开记录中存在持续的上游依赖性。在官方追踪器上，464份XSA中有113份影响Qubes；在主要标签下，109份QSBs（79.8%）归因于Xen、CPU/微架构或其他上游组件，而非Qubes-core逻辑，加权视图下结果相似。变化点分析将2015年第一季度识别为季度公告系列的主要断裂点，而2018年后的年度披露率在统计上保持平稳。泊松推断在分散诊断和负二项式敏感性检验下保持稳定。归因代码簿在30-QSB分层审计中表现良好，S型VDMs在描述性上拟合良好，但在短期预测中并未显著优于滚动均值基线。总体而言，Qubes的公开公告记录看似稳定，但并非平静：披露活动在高于早期年份的水平上达到平台期，而观察到的负担仍然集中在上游信任锚点上。

articles.title: Penny: Transition Network Analysis of Learner-Chatbot Interactions in Scaffolded EFL Writing
articles.abstract: Generative AI chatbots promise to transform English as a Foreign Language (EFL) writing by providing immediate, personalised feedback. However, their pedagogical value depends on how learners engage with them - a process often treated as a "black box." This study uses Transition Network Analysis to model the temporal dynamics of Japanese EFL learners using "Penny," an LLM-powered writing chatbot. Analysis of over 4,500 writing sessions and 21,000 chatbot interactions reveals two dominant behavioural loops: a "Revision Loop," where feedback leads directly to successful error correction, and a "Chat Loop," where learners engage in sustained dialogue with the chatbot following feedback. Crucially, EFL proficiency significantly shapes interaction: high-proficiency learners engage more in open dialogue and negotiation with the chatbot, while low-proficiency learners rely more heavily on repetitive corrective feedback cycles. The findings demonstrate that AI-scaffolded writing is a non-linear, dialogic process and highlight the need for differentiated chatbot design to move beyond simple error correction and foster deeper cognitive engagement for all learners.
articles.titleCn: Penny：支架式英语写作中学习者-聊天机器人交互的转换网络分析
articles.abstractCn: 生成式人工智能聊天机器人承诺通过提供即时、个性化的反馈来改变外语（EFL）写作。然而，它们的教学价值取决于学习者如何与之互动——这一过程通常被视为一个“黑箱”。本研究使用转换网络分析来模拟日本EFL学习者使用“Penny”（一个由大型语言模型驱动的写作聊天机器人）时的时序动态。对超过4,500次写作会话和21,000次聊天机器人互动的分析揭示了两种主导的行为循环：一个“修订循环”，其中反馈直接导致成功的错误纠正，以及一个“聊天循环”，其中学习者在收到反馈后与聊天机器人进行持续的对话。关键在于，EFL水平显著影响着互动：高水平学习者在与聊天机器人进行开放式对话和协商方面更为积极，而低水平学习者则更依赖重复性的纠正反馈循环。研究结果表明，人工智能辅助写作是一个非线性、对话性的过程，并强调了需要设计差异化的聊天机器人，以超越简单的错误纠正，并促进所有学习者的深度认知参与。

articles.title: MARS: Multi-hop Adaptive Retrieval and SPARQL Generation for KGQA
articles.abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated strong reasoning performance, but their tendency to hallucinate limits their reliability in knowledge-intensive tasks requiring up-to-date and grounded information. Combining knowledge graphs (KGs) with LLMs facilitates the use of explicit symbolic knowledge that can be continuously updated without costly fine-tuning, while benefiting from rapidly advancing LLM reasoning. We propose MARS, a scalable knowledge graph question answering (KGQA) approach that requires no model fine-tuning. Rather than relying on open-ended agentic exploration, MARS performs a structured retrieval procedure that links question entities to the KG and iteratively retrieves relevant next-hop information. At each step, MARS decides whether to continue graph traversal or to generate the final SPARQL query, allowing the model to adapt the retrieval depth to the question while keeping the overall pipeline more predictable than fully agentic approaches. We evaluate MARS on three established KGQA benchmarks across several LLMs and settings, including multilingual evaluation, and provide insights through ablation studies and error analysis. Our approach achieves competitive performance relative to state-of-the-art methods while remaining efficient and scalable. The evaluation results, code and resources are publicly available: https://github.com/dice-group/mars-kgqa.
articles.titleCn: MARS：用于知识图谱问答的多跳自适应检索和SPARQL生成
articles.abstractCn: 大型语言模型（LLMs）已展现出强大的推理能力，但它们产生幻觉的倾向限制了它们在需要最新且可靠信息的知识密集型任务中的可靠性。将知识图谱（KGs）与LLMs结合，可以方便地利用可持续更新且无需昂贵微调的显式符号知识，同时受益于快速发展的LLM推理能力。我们提出了MARS，一种无需模型微调的可扩展知识图谱问答（KGQA）方法。MARS不是依赖开放式代理探索，而是执行结构化的检索过程，将问题实体链接到KG，并迭代检索相关的下一跳信息。在每一步，MARS决定是继续图遍历还是生成最终的SPARQL查询，使模型能够根据问题调整检索深度，同时使整体流程比完全代理式方法更具可预测性。我们在多个LLMs和设置下，包括多语言评估，在三个已建立的知识图谱问答（KGQA）基准上评估了MARS，并通过消融研究和错误分析提供了见解。我们的方法在性能上与最先进的方法具有竞争力，同时保持了高效和可扩展性。评估结果、代码和资源公开可用：https://github.com/dice-group/mars-kgqa。

articles.title: Answer-Conditioned Chains of Thought Degrade Verifiable-Reasoning Distillation in Large Language Models
articles.abstract: A standard recipe for distilling the reasoning ability of large language models (LLMs) is to sample chains of thought from the model, keep those that reach the correct final answer, and fine-tune on the survivors. When sampling fails, a common fix shows the generator the gold answer and asks it to write a chain that reaches that answer. We show that this second step degrades the training data in a way that correctness filtering cannot catch. We run a controlled experiment that fixes the generator, the problem set, and the correctness filter, and varies only whether the chain is generated under answer-conditioning, the gold answer shown with a request to reach it. Training a strong instruction-tuned reasoning model on its own answer-conditioned chains sharply lowers its verifiable-reasoning accuracy. The loss grows with difficulty, reaching as much as about 27 points on the hardest competition problems. The mechanism is legible in the chains themselves, which rationalize backward from the shown answer instead of deriving it, with the early final-answer statement as the measurable symptom. The harm is a property of the data rather than the generator, read off unlabeled generations before any fine-tuning, ordering the penalty across eight thinking models from four families, and transferring across teacher families. A prompt ablation localizes it to the rationalize-toward instruction rather than the answer's bare visibility. The practical takeaway is to generate answer-blind, because no correctness filter can see this damage in the data.
articles.titleCn: 答案-条件化思维链会损害大型语言模型中可验证推理蒸馏
articles.abstractCn: 蒸馏大型语言模型（LLM）推理能力的一种标准方法是从模型中采样思维链，保留那些得到正确最终答案的链，并对这些幸存者进行微调。当采样失败时，常见的修复方法是将正确答案展示给生成器，并要求它编写一个达到该答案的思维链。我们证明，第二步以某种方式降低了训练数据，这种降低方式正确性筛选无法捕捉。我们进行了一项受控实验，固定了生成器、问题集和正确性筛选器，仅变化思维链是在答案条件下生成，还是展示正确答案并要求达到该答案。在自身答案条件下的链上训练强大的指令微调推理模型会显著降低其可验证推理准确率。损失随着难度增加而增长，在最难的竞赛问题中达到约27分。该机制在思维链本身中可见，它们从展示的答案中反向推理，而不是推导出答案，其中早期最终答案陈述是可测量的症状。这种危害是数据本身的属性，而非生成器，从任何微调前的未标记生成中读取，按顺序对来自四个系列的八个思维模型施加惩罚，并在教师系列之间转移。提示消融将其局限于向答案反向推理的指令，而不是答案的简单可见性。实际启示是生成答案盲的，因为没有任何正确性筛选器能在数据中看到这种损害。

articles.title: CityLLM: A framework for natural-language querying of semantic 3D city models
articles.abstract: Semantic 3D city models provide rich geometric and semantic information, but remain challenging for non-experts and interdisciplinary researchers to access and query due to their complex structures and specialized data formats. To address this issue, we present CityLLM, a framework for natural-language querying of semantic 3D city models alongside complementary urban datasets. The framework combines spatial and graph databases within an LLM-based workflow that supports iterative query refinement and cross-database chaining. We evaluate CityLLM on a CityJSON dataset of Rotterdam (853 LoD2 buildings) using GPT-OSS, Gemini 3.1, and GPT-5.4, along with selected variants, across multiple metrics: answer correctness, visualization correctness, query success, and retry attempts. A total of 54 natural-language queries are curated across four scenarios: spatial, graph, cross-database, and conversational. Results show strong overall performance, with answer correctness ranging from 85.2% to 100%, visualization correctness from 92.9% to 100%, a 100% query success rate, and fewer than three retries across all 54 queries. Overall, the findings suggest that CityLLM provides a lightweight and extensible approach for conversational access to semantic 3D city data.
articles.comment: Accepted to the 21st International 3D GeoInfo Conference. To appear in the ISPRS Annals of the Photogrammetry, Remote Sensing and Spatial Information Sciences
articles.titleCn: CityLLM：一个用于语义3D城市模型的自然语言查询框架
articles.abstractCn: 语义3D城市模型提供了丰富的几何和语义信息，但由于其复杂结构和专业数据格式，对非专家和跨学科研究人员来说，访问和查询仍然具有挑战性。为解决这一问题，我们提出了CityLLM，一个用于自然语言查询语义3D城市模型及配套城市数据集的框架。该框架将空间数据库和图数据库结合在一个基于LLM的工作流程中，支持迭代查询优化和跨数据库链式查询。我们使用包含鹿特丹（853个LoD2建筑）的CityJSON数据集，通过GPT-OSS、Gemini 3.1和GPT-5.4及其选定变体，在多个指标上评估CityLLM：答案正确性、可视化正确性、查询成功率和重试次数。共设计了54个自然语言查询，涵盖四个场景：空间查询、图查询、跨数据库查询和对话式查询。结果表明，CityLLM表现出优异的整体性能，答案正确性在85.2%至100%之间，可视化正确性在92.9%至100%之间，查询成功率100%，所有54个查询中重试次数均少于三次。总体而言，研究结果表明CityLLM为对话式访问语义3D城市数据提供了一种轻量级且可扩展的方法。

articles.title: xHC: Expanded Hyper-Connections
articles.abstract: Hyper-Connections (HC) expand the residual stream of Transformers into $N$ parallel streams, providing a form of memory scaling beyond model width and depth. Manifold-Constrained HC (mHC) stabilizes this formulation at scale. The large gains from $N{=}1$ to $N{=}4$ suggest residual-stream expansion as a promising scaling axis. However, existing HC-family methods typically stop at $N{=}4$. Our experiments reveal why: scaling mHC beyond this point yields diminishing performance gains and rapidly increasing training cost. We attribute this limitation to two bottlenecks: insufficient write-back information for an expanding number of streams and residual-mixing generation whose cost scales cubically with $N$. To address both bottlenecks, we propose xHC (Expanded Hyper-Connections), the first HC-family method to achieve meaningful expansion beyond $N{=}4$. xHC combines temporal feature augmentation for richer write-back with a sparse residual-stream architecture that updates only $k=4$ of the $N=16$ streams while retaining dense access to the full residual state. Across 18B and 28B MoE models, xHC delivers strong and consistent downstream improvements. On an 18B MoE model, xHC improves the average downstream score by 4.0 points over mHC, while adding only modest training FLOPs over the vanilla baseline. Scaling-law experiments show that the vanilla and mHC require $1.50\times$ and $1.19\times$ the compute of xHC, respectively, to reach the same loss. Practical large-$N$ training also requires controlling memory traffic from the expanded residual state. We therefore introduce xHC-Flash, which reduces the per-sublayer memory traffic from $73.5C$ to $40C$, comparable to the $34C$ required by mHC at $N{=}4$, while retaining the gains of full xHC. Together, xHC and xHC-Flash make large-$N$ residual-stream expansion effective and practical for LLM pre-training.
articles.titleCn: xHC：扩展超连接
articles.abstractCn: 超连接（HC）将Transformer的残差流扩展为$N$个并行流，提供了一种超越模型宽度和深度的内存扩展形式。约束流形HC（mHC）使这种形式在规模上稳定。从$N=1$到$N=4$的显著增益表明残差流扩展是一个有前景的扩展轴。然而，现有的HC系列方法通常止步于$N=4$。我们的实验揭示了原因：在这一点上扩展mHC会导致性能提升递减和训练成本急剧增加。我们将此限制归因于两个瓶颈：为扩展的流提供不足的写回信息，以及残差混合生成的成本随$N$的三次方增长。为解决这两个瓶颈，我们提出了xHC（扩展超连接），这是首个在$N>4$时实现有意义扩展的HC系列方法。xHC结合了时间特征增强以实现更丰富的写回，并采用稀疏残差流架构，仅更新$k=4$个$N=16$个流中的流，同时保留对完整残差状态的密集访问。在18B和28B MoE模型上，xHC提供了强大且一致的下游改进。在18B MoE模型上，xHC将平均下游分数提高了4.0分，而仅比基础线模型增加了适度的训练FLOPs。缩放律实验表明，基础线和mHC分别需要xHC的$1.50\times$和$1.19\times$的计算量才能达到相同的损失。实际的大$N$训练还需要控制从扩展残差状态的记忆流量。因此，我们引入了xHC-Flash，它将每层子模块的记忆流量从$73.5C$减少到$40C$，与mHC在$N=4$时所需的$34C$相当，同时保留了完整xHC的增益。总而言之，xHC和xHC-Flash使大$N$残差流扩展对LLM预训练有效且实用。

articles.title: Controlled Reformulation Testing for Logical Consistency in Large Language Models
articles.abstract: Large language models (LLMs) frequently contradict themselves when the surface form of a logically equivalent question changes. We present a benchmark of 350 question families (1,750 total questions) for Controlled Reformulation Testing (CRTBench) to evaluate logical invariance. In this benchmark, we investigate LLMs' ability to maintain consistent answers across controlled reformulations, which include contrapositive rewriting, double negation, negation flipping, and passive voice. We evaluate several frontier LLMs and observe an accuracy-consistency gap where GPT-5.4-mini achieves $98.9\%$ base accuracy but only $60.3\%$ family-level consistency, while reasoning-optimized o4-mini achieves $96.9\%$ consistency. From our experiments, we observe that failures cluster around logically nontrivial transformations such as contrapositive rewriting ($72.4\%$ for GPT-5.4-mini) and double negation ($84.6\%$), while surface-level rephrasing remains robust ($94-100\%$). Increasing reasoning effort improves GPT-5.4-mini to $85.4\%$ consistency, but leaves GPT-5.4 unchanged overall because gains on nested negation are offset by failures on quantifier families. These results show that accuracy alone is not enough for evaluating logical reasoning in LLMs.
articles.titleCn: 大型语言模型中的逻辑一致性控制式重述测试
articles.abstractCn: 大型语言模型（LLMs）在逻辑等价问题的表层形式发生变化时，经常出现自我矛盾。我们提出了一个包含350个问题家族（共1,750个问题）的基准测试（CRTBench），用于控制性重述测试，以评估逻辑不变性。在这个基准测试中，我们研究了LLMs在受控重述下保持一致答案的能力，这些重述包括逆否命题重写、双重否定、否定翻转和被动语态。我们对多个前沿LLMs进行了评估，观察到准确性与一致性之间的差距：GPT-5.4-mini实现了98.9%的基础准确率，但家族级别的仅达到60.3%，而推理优化的o4-mini达到了96.9%的一致性。从我们的实验中，我们发现错误主要集中在逻辑上非平凡的转换，如逆否命题重写（GPT-5.4-mini为72.4%）和双重否定（84.6%），而表层重述仍然稳健（94-100%）。增加推理努力将GPT-5.4-mini的一致性提升至85.4%，但GPT-5.4整体未发生变化，因为嵌套否定的收益被量化家族中的失败所抵消。这些结果表明，仅凭准确率不足以评估LLMs中的逻辑推理能力。

articles.title: WrAFT: a Modularized Automated Writing Evaluation System for Argumentative Essays
articles.abstract: This study presents WrAFT, a Writing Assessment and Feedback Tool, that delivers both accurate and reliable scores and effective comprehensive feedback to argumentative essays. WrAFT adopts a modular design by dividing automated writing evaluation (AWE) tasks into scoring, surface-level feedback, and deep-level feedback. In building the system, various Large Language Models (LLMs) have been evaluated, including LLaMA-3.3-70B-Instruct, GPT-4o, and Claude 3.7, through both direct prompting and supervised fine-tuning approaches. A proprietary dataset of 480 TOEFL Independent Writing essays with official benchmark scores was utilized. Benchmark-based evaluation shows that WrAFT achieves state-of-the-art performance in scoring, with a quadratic weighted kappa (QWK) of 0.84 and a root mean square error (RMSE) of 0.44 against official scores on a scale of 0-5. Human evaluation of system-generated feedback also reveals high approval ratings: 96.14 percent for surface-level feedback, 93.03 percent for deep-level macro feedback, and 94.69 percent for deep-level micro feedback. An interactive user interface has been developed for the system and is publicly available and free to use.
articles.titleCn: WrAFT：一种用于议论文的模块化自动写作评估系统
articles.abstractCn: 本研究介绍了WrAFT，一个写作评估与反馈工具，它能够为议论文提供准确可靠的评分和有效的综合反馈。WrAFT采用模块化设计，将自动化写作评估（AWE）任务划分为评分、表层反馈和深层反馈。在系统构建过程中，通过直接提示和监督微调方法，评估了多种大型语言模型（LLM），包括LLaMA-3.3-70B-Instruct、GPT-4o和Claude 3.7。研究使用了包含480篇托福独立写作作文及官方基准分数的专有数据集。基于基准的评估显示，WrAFT在评分方面达到了最先进的性能，与0-5分制官方分数相比，其二次加权kappa系数（QWK）为0.84，均方根误差（RMSE）为0.44。系统生成的反馈的人工评估也显示出极高的认可度：表层反馈的认可度为96.14%，深层宏观反馈的认可度为93.03%，深层微观反馈的认可度为94.69%。该系统已开发出交互式用户界面，并公开免费使用。

articles.title: RetroAgent: Harnessing LLMs to Search Over Structured Memory for Agentic Retrosynthesis Planning
articles.abstract: Multi-step retrosynthesis planning seeks to decompose a target molecule into commercially available building blocks through a sequence of feasible reactions. The vast combinatorial search space makes this task challenging even for expert chemists. Traditional methods combine tree search with offline-trained value networks that score candidates in isolation, without reasoning about complete multi-step routes. Recent work leverages Large Language Models (LLMs) for this task, but relies on simple interfaces that limit exploration of the full search space. We introduce RetroAgent, an LLM agent that bridges symbolic search and neural reasoning through a harness with structured memory. Through memory and chemistry tools, the agent observes the full search state, including explored routes, available alternatives, and properties of intermediates, enabling informed decisions grounded in both global progress and domain knowledge. Experiments on in-distribution and out-of-distribution benchmarks demonstrate that RetroAgent delivers strong performance and generalization.
articles.titleCn: RetroAgent：利用大型语言模型搜索结构化记忆以实现自主逆合成规划
articles.abstractCn: 多步逆合成规划旨在通过一系列可行的反应将目标分子分解为市售的构建模块。庞大的组合搜索空间使得这项任务对专家化学家来说也充满挑战。传统方法结合树搜索与离线训练的价值网络对候选分子进行评分，但并未对完整的多步路线进行推理。近期的研究利用大型语言模型（LLM）处理这项任务，但依赖于简单的接口，限制了全面探索搜索空间。我们介绍了RetroAgent，这是一个通过结构化记忆连接符号搜索和神经推理的LLM代理。通过记忆和化学工具，该代理观察完整的搜索状态，包括已探索的路线、可用的替代方案以及中间体的性质，从而基于全局进展和领域知识做出明智决策。在分布内和分布外基准测试上的实验表明，RetroAgent展现出强大的性能和泛化能力。

articles.title: Manufactured Divisiveness: Decomposing the Hostile Content of Seven Social Media Influence Operations
articles.abstract: State-backed influence operations are routinely measured as high-prevalence sources of ``hate'' and ``toxicity.'' We argue those rates rest on a measurement error: the detectors behind them are validated to catch a broader definition inclusive of hostility or divisiveness aimed at an out-group, and so over-attribute hate to content better described as partisan or geopolitical invective. Across 25.08M tweets from seven government-attributed campaigns in the Twitter Information Operations archive (8,275 accounts), we separate hate from the other forms of divisiveness. We first validate a two-prompt LLM-based detector, matching human labels at Cohen's $κ=0.82$, to identify the broader hostility; we then develop an auditable rule, agreeing with an expert at $κ=0.52$, to further classify this content (5,457 posts) into three sub-categories. About 50.1% are identity-based attacks on people, whereas 30.4% are partisan attacks and 19.5% invective against states and their foreign policy. Reporting all of it as hate therefore overstates hate roughly twofold; only 18.7% is both identity-based and dehumanizing or inciting. Six of seven campaigns sort into three regimes that a single ``hate'' rate flattens, namely identity hate (RU-op and IRA, both Russia-attributed), geopolitical invective (both Iran operations), and partisan divisiveness (both Venezuela operations). We call the shared product $manufactured divisiveness$. The line to separate these constructs itself remains unsettled: on the hardest cases three independent human experts agree only moderately (pairwise $κ=0.37$--$0.50$), and the best of nineteen LLM models tops out at $κ=0.601$ against the experts' majority. Our findings can help redefine the study of hate in the context of influence campaigns and broader online discourse.
articles.titleCn: 制造分裂：分解七个社交媒体影响力行动的敌对内容
articles.abstractCn: 国家支持的干预行动通常被测量为高发“仇恨”和“毒性”的来源。我们认为这些比率基于一种测量误差：其背后的检测器被验证用于捕捉包含针对外群体的敌意或分裂性的更广泛定义，因此过度将仇恨归因于更适合描述为党派或地缘政治谩骂的内容。在推特信息行动档案中来自七个被归因于政府的活动（8,275个账号）的2.508亿推文中，我们将仇恨与其他形式的分裂性内容区分开来。我们首先验证了一个基于LLM的双提示检测器，其与人类标签的Cohen's $κ=0.82$相匹配，以识别更广泛的敌意；然后我们开发了一个可审计的规则，与专家的Cohen's $κ=0.52$相一致，以进一步将这些内容（5,457篇帖子）分为三个子类别。其中约50.1%是针对个人的基于身份的攻击，30.4%是党派攻击，19.5%是针对国家和其外交政策的谩骂。因此将所有这些内容报告为仇恨会大致夸大仇恨的两倍；只有18.7%既是基于身份的又是非人化或煽动性的。七个活动中有六个被分为三个体系，一个单一的“仇恨”比率会使这些体系变得模糊，即身份仇恨（RU-op和IRA，均被归因于俄罗斯）、地缘政治谩骂（两个伊朗行动）和党派分裂性（两个委内瑞拉行动）。我们将这种共同产品称为“制造分裂性”。区分这些结构的界限本身仍未确定：在最困难的案例中，三个独立的人类专家的共识仅中度（成对Cohen's $κ=0.37$--$0.50$），而十九个LLM模型中最好的模型与专家多数的Cohen's $κ$最高达到0.601。我们的研究有助于在影响活动和更广泛的在线话语背景下重新定义仇恨的研究。

articles.title: LLM Evaluators are Biased across Languages

authors.name: Lucas Resck
authors.affiliation: trained reward models and prompted LLM-as-a-Judge

articles.abstract: LLM evaluators (trained reward models and prompted LLM-as-a-Judge) are routinely validated via pairwise accuracy. In a multilingual setting, this operates under the premise that high pairwise accuracy implies reliable, language-neutral scoring. We show that this assumption does not hold. We conduct experiments with semantically identical instruction-response pairs across 23 languages, and find that multilingual evaluators assign significantly different scores to different evaluation languages. The bias is statistically significant and consistent across eight open-weight evaluators of different architectures and training paradigms, persists in frontier judges, and is strongly correlated with language resource level: lower-resource languages are scored more generously. Meanwhile, these biases are invisible to pairwise accuracy: evaluators achieve above 90% pairwise accuracy, yet have up to 43% difference in acceptance rate across languages under a global decision threshold, meaning, for instance, that harmful content in lower-resource languages is more likely to pass safety filters. Per-language thresholds would require language identification, which can be defeated by code-switched prompts. We then investigate why lower-resource languages receive higher rather than lower scores, and we find that model uncertainty is linked with the effect: models tend to give higher scores when less confident, both under negative log-likelihood and under token-free uncertainty measures; however, language identity remains a significant predictor after controlling for uncertainty, and the bias cannot be explained away by content difficulty alone, but is a structural, language-level misalignment.
articles.titleCn: 大型语言模型评估器在不同语言之间存在偏见
articles.abstractCn: LLM评估器（包括经过训练的奖励模型和作为裁判的提示LLM）通常通过成对准确率进行验证。在多语言环境下，这种做法基于一个前提，即高成对准确率意味着可靠且语言中立的评分。我们证明这一假设并不成立。我们通过在23种语言中测试语义相同的指令-响应对进行了实验，发现多语言评估器对不同评估语言的评分存在显著差异。这种偏差在统计上显著，并且在八种不同架构和训练范式下的开放权重评估器中保持一致，即使在前沿裁判中也存在，并且与语言资源水平高度相关：低资源语言得到的评分更为宽容。与此同时，这些偏差对成对准确率来说是不可见的：评估器可以达到90%以上的成对准确率，但在全局决策阈值下，不同语言之间的接受率差异高达43%，这意味着例如低资源语言中的有害内容更有可能通过安全过滤器。需要为每种语言设置单独阈值，但这需要语言识别，而语言切换提示可以规避这一要求。我们进一步调查了为什么低资源语言会得到更高的评分而不是更低的评分，发现模型的不确定性与此效应相关：当模型信心不足时，无论是负对数似然还是无标记的不确定性度量下，模型倾向于给出更高的评分；然而，在控制不确定性后，语言身份仍然是一个显著的预测因素，这种偏差不能仅用内容难度来解释，而是一种结构性的、语言层面的错位。
